input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Shutdown System`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Shutdown System` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.7.2.2
            TITLE: when the shutdown circuit opens:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.2.2: When the Shutdown Circuit Opens:
EV.7.2.2 When the Shutdown Circuit Opens: 
a. The Tractive System must Shutdown 
b. All Accumulator current flow must stop immediately  EV.5.4.3

c. The voltage in the Tractive System must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2 in five seconds or less 
d. The Motor(s) must spin free.  Torque must not be applied to the Motor(s)

RULE: ev.8.3.1
            TITLE: the charging shutdown circuit consists of:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.8.3.1: The Charging Shutdown Circuit consists of:
EV.8.3.1 The Charging Shutdown Circuit consists of: 
a. Charger Shutdown Button  EV.8.2.7 
b. Accumulator Management System (AMS)  EV.7.3 
c. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD)  EV.7.6

RULE: ev.7.2.3
            TITLE: when the ams, imd or bspd open the shutdown circuit:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.2.3: When the AMS, IMD or BSPD Open the Shutdown Circuit:
EV.7.2.3 When the AMS, IMD or BSPD Open the Shutdown Circuit: 
a. The Tractive System must stay disabled until manually reset  
b. The Tractive System must be reset only by manual action of a person directly at the 
vehicle  
c. The driver must not be able to reactivate the Tractive System from inside the vehicle  
d. Operation of the Shutdown Buttons or TSMS must not reset the Shutdown Circuit

RULE: ev.9.2
            TITLE: low voltage (glv) system

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.2: Low Voltage (GLV) System
EV.9.2 Low Voltage (GLV) System 
The Shutdown Circuit may be Closed when or after the GLV System is energized  EV.7.2.4

RULE: ev.7.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:
EV.7.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series: 
a. Accumulator Management System (AMS)  EV.7.3 
b. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD)  EV.7.6
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 101 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  EV.7.7 
d. Interlocks (as required)  EV.7.8 
e. Master Switches (GLVMS, TSMS)  EV.7.9 
f. Shutdown Buttons  EV.7.10 
g. Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
h. Inertia Switch  T.9.4

RULE: ic.9.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:
IC.9.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:  
a. Primary Master Switch  IC.9.3 
b. Cockpit Main Switch  IC.9.4 
c. (ETC Only) Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  IC.4.8 
d. Brake Overtravel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
e. Inertia Switch (if used)  T.9.4

RULE: ev.7.2.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit must open when any of the following exist:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.2.1: The Shutdown Circuit must Open when any of the following exist:
EV.7.2.1 The Shutdown Circuit must Open when any of the following exist:   
a. Operation of, or detection from any of the components listed in EV.7.1.1 
b. Any shutdown of the GLV System

RULE: ev.8.4.2
            TITLE: when the charging shutdown circuit opens:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.8.4.2: When the Charging Shutdown Circuit Opens:
EV.8.4.2 When the Charging Shutdown Circuit Opens: 
a. All current flow to the Accumulator must stop immediately 
b. The voltage in the Tractive System must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2 in five seconds or less 
c. The Charger must be turned off  
d. The Charger must stay disabled until manually reset

RULE: ev.7.10.6
            TITLE: pressing any of the shutdown buttons must open the shutdown circuit  ev.7.2.2

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.10.6: Pressing any of the Shutdown Buttons must Open the Shutdown Circuit  EV.7.2.2
EV.7.10.6 Pressing any of the Shutdown Buttons must Open the Shutdown Circuit  EV.7.2.2

RULE: ev.9.3.2
            TITLE: tractive system active must not be possible until both:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.3.2: Tractive System Active must not be possible until both:
EV.9.3.2 Tractive System Active must not be possible until both: 
• GLV System is Energized 
• Shutdown Circuit is Closed
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 107 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: ev.7.9.3
            TITLE: the tractive system master switch (tsms) must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.9.3: The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:
EV.7.9.3 The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:  
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit in the OFF position  EV.7.2.2  
b. Be the last switch before the AIRs except for Precharge circuitry and Interlocks.   
c. Be in the center of a completely orange circular area of > 50 mm in diameter 
d. Be labeled “TS” and the symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black 
lightning bolt on yellow background). 
e. Be fitted with a "lockout/tagout" capability in the OFF position  EV.11.3.1

RULE: ic.4.8.4
            TITLE: when any of the above conditions exist, the bspd must open the shutdown circuit ic.9.2.2

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.8.4: When any of the above conditions exist, the BSPD must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2
IC.4.8.4 When any of the above conditions exist, the BSPD must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2

RULE: ic.9.2.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit must open upon operation of, or detection from any of the components

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.2.1: The Shutdown Circuit must Open upon operation of, or detection from any of the components
IC.9.2.1 The Shutdown Circuit must Open upon operation of, or detection from any of the components 
listed in IC.9.1.1

RULE: ev.7.1.2
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit must directly carry the current driving the accumulator isolation relays

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.2: The Shutdown Circuit must directly carry the current driving the Accumulator Isolation Relays
EV.7.1.2 The Shutdown Circuit must directly carry the current driving the Accumulator Isolation Relays 
(AIRs) and the Precharge Circuit Relay.

RULE: ev.7.1.5
            TITLE: the shutdown buttons, bots, tsms, glvms and interlocks must directly carry the shutdown

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.5: The Shutdown Buttons, BOTS, TSMS, GLVMS and Interlocks must directly carry the Shutdown
EV.7.1.5 The Shutdown Buttons, BOTS, TSMS, GLVMS and Interlocks must directly carry the Shutdown 
Circuit current

RULE: ev.7.1.6
            TITLE: the team must be able to demonstrate all features and functions of the shutdown circuit and

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.6: The team must be able to demonstrate all features and functions of the Shutdown Circuit and
EV.7.1.6 The team must be able to demonstrate all features and functions of the Shutdown Circuit and 
components at Electrical Technical Inspection.

RULE: ev.7.10.1
            TITLE: three shutdown buttons must be installed on the vehicle.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.10.1: Three Shutdown Buttons must be installed on the vehicle.
EV.7.10.1 Three Shutdown Buttons must be installed on the vehicle.

RULE: ic.9.2.2
            TITLE: when the shutdown circuit opens, it must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.2.2: When the Shutdown Circuit Opens, it must:
IC.9.2.2 When the Shutdown Circuit Opens, it must: 
a. Stop the engine 
b. Disconnect power to the: 
• Fuel Pump(s) 
• Ignition 
• (ETC only) Electronic Throttle  IC.4.1.1

RULE: ev.7.10.2
            TITLE: each shutdown button must be a push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.10.2: Each Shutdown Button must be a push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch
EV.7.10.2 Each Shutdown Button must be a push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch

RULE: ev.7.1.4
            TITLE: the ams, imd and bspd must have completely independent circuits to open the shutdown

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.4: The AMS, IMD and BSPD must have completely independent circuits to Open the Shutdown
EV.7.1.4 The AMS, IMD and BSPD must have completely independent circuits to Open the Shutdown 
Circuit. 
The design of the respective circuits must ensure that a failure cannot result in electrical 
power being fed back into the Shutdown Circuit.
